title: Subduction erosion and basal friction along the sediment-starved convergent margin off Antofagasta, Chile
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Prestack depth-migrated seismic records image slope debris that collects in a frontal prism, including approximately 30% pore fluid, which fills subducting grabens and is subsequently incorporated into an approximately 1.5-km-thick interplate reflective layer. Two mechanisms of basal erosion are identified from seismic images: erosion by seamount tunnelling and removal of large rock lenses of a distending upper plate. The study examines how subduction erosion is associated with strong interplate coupling and upper plate abrasion along the sediment-starved north Chile margin.

location.description: Offshore Antofagasta, north Chilean margin at ~23°S; erosive convergent margin

data.name: CINCA95 Multichannel Seismic Reflection Data — Antofagasta Transect
data.description: Prestack depth-migrated MCS reflection profiles from SO104 (CINCA95) offshore Antofagasta; images subducting grabens, frontal prism, 1.5-km thick interplate reflective layer, and seamount tunnelling structures. No direct public dataset download identified; BGR holds track shapefiles only (Marine_Seismik@bgr.de).

data.name: CINCA95 Swath Bathymetry — North Chile Margin
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SO104 cruise offshore Antofagasta used to characterize slope morphology and tectonic features. Published as Geersen et al. (2018), merged multibeam grid from SO104, SO244, and MGL1610.

data.name: CINCA95 Free-Air Gravity — North Chile
data.description: Shipboard gravity data from CINCA95 cruise used to supplement seismic interpretation of crustal structure

data.name: CINCA95 Navigation Data
data.description: Navigation and positioning data from the SO104 cruise

_data_note: CINCA95 (SO104) data hosted at MGDS. Prestack depth migration applied to MCS profiles makes them PROCESSED.

processing.summary: Multichannel seismic reflection data from the 1995 CINCA95 cruise (R/V SONNE SO104) were processed using prestack depth migration (PSDM). The processing yields detailed depth images of the sediment-starved north Chilean margin near Antofagasta (~23°30'S). Key structural features resolved include subducting oceanic grabens filled with slope debris (~30% pore fluid), a ~1.5-km-thick interplate reflective layer, and evidence of basal erosion by seamount tunnelling and upper plate distension.
workflow: 4. Velocity analysis and tomographic velocity model building
processing.notes: PSDM applied; this is a fully processed section used for tectonic interpretation

analysis_notes: Open access PDF available via OceanRep GEOMAR. Data from CINCA95 SO104 cruise (1995), accessible via MGDS. The Semantic Scholar record confirms OA PDF at oceanrep.geomar.de.
